terms.definition: An algorithm that builds a solution by making the locally optimal choice at each step.
The condition that a globally optimal solution can be reached by making a locally optimal choice at each step.
The property that an optimal solution to a problem contains optimal solutions to its sub-problems.
Classic provably-optimal greedy problem: select the maximum number of non-overlapping intervals by sorting on end time.
Knapsack variant where fractional items are allowed; provably solvable greedily by sorting on value/weight ratio.
A greedy algorithm for prefix-free coding: repeatedly merge the two lowest-frequency nodes; produces optimal symbol codes.
Generation strategy that picks the argmax-probability next token at each step; fastest but not guaranteed to produce the highest-probability sequence.
A strategy that aims for a good-enough solution without a correctness proof; many practical greedies are heuristics.
